title: IELTS General Training Mock Test 1
description: Complete IELTS General Training test with all 4 skills
instructions: Read the instructions for each part of the paper carefully. Answer all the questions. Write your answers in the answer sheet. You must complete the answer sheet within the time limit. At the end of the test, hand in both this question paper and your answer sheet.

words.example: The Earth formed approximately 4.5 billion years ago.

words.definition: To be composed or made up of
words.example: The Solar System consists of eight planets.
The Earth's surface is 71% water.
The idea of months originated from the moon.
The biosphere includes all habitats on Earth.

words.definition: To change from one form to another
words.example: Cyanobacteria convert sunlight to oxygen.
The Earth has a diameter of about 12,742 km.
The Earth rotates on its axis once a day.
Advantage Sales & Marketing offers extensive services.
Chipotle offers a flexible work schedule.
The Vitamin Shoppe has progressive policies.
PSA Healthcare provides compassionate care.

words.definition: A person being considered for a position
words.example: Candidates must have experience.
There are room for advancement opportunities.
PSA Healthcare provides comprehensive services.
Fire drills prepare you for emergencies.
You must evacuate during a fire.
Fire is a major hazard.
People must respond quickly to fire alarms.

words.definition: A measure taken to prevent something
words.example: Fire drills are an important precaution.

reading.title: General Training Reading Mock 1 - All Question Types

sections.title: Reading Passage 1: The Earth
sections.description: Questions 1-14 - Types: Matching Choice, MCQ, Trinary (True/False), Headings Match

content.text: The Earth is the third planet from the Sun and it is the only planet known to have life on it. The Earth formed around 4.5 billion years ago. It is one of four rocky planets on the inside of the Solar System. The other three are Mercury, Venus, and Mars.

The large mass of the Sun makes the Earth move around it, just as the mass of the Earth makes the Moon move around it. The Earth also turns round in space, so different parts face the Sun at different times. The Earth goes around the Sun once (one 'year') for every 365¼ times it turns all the way around (one 'day').

The Moon goes around the Earth about every 27⅓ days, and reflects light from the Sun. As the Earth goes round the Sun at the same time, the changing light of the Moon takes about 29½ days to go from dark to bright to dark again. That is where the idea of 'month' came from. However, now most months have 30 or 31 days so they fit into one year.

The Earth is the only planet in our Solar System that has a large amount of liquid water. About 71% of the surface of the Earth is covered by oceans. Because of this, it is sometimes called the 'Blue Planet'.

Because of its water, the Earth is home to millions of species of plants and animals. The things that live on Earth have changed its surface greatly. For example, early cyanobacteria changed the air and gave it oxygen. The living part of the Earth's surface is called the 'biosphere'.

The Earth is part of the eight planets and many thousands of small bodies that move around the Sun as its Solar System. The Solar System is moving through the Orion Arm of the Milky Way Galaxy now, and will be for about the next 10,000 years.

The Earth is generally 150,000,000 kilometers or 93,000,000 miles away from the Sun (this distance is named an 'Astronomical Unit'). The Earth moves along its way at an average speed of about 30 km or 19 mi a second. The Earth turns all the way around about 365¼ times in the time it takes for the Earth to go all the way around the Sun. To make up this extra bit of a day every year, an additional day is used every four years. This is named a 'leap year'.

The Moon goes around the Earth at an average distance of 400,000 kilometers (250,000 mi). It is locked to Earth, so that it always has the same half facing the Earth; the other half is called the 'dark side of the Moon'.

passages.title: Company Profiles
content: The Vitamin Shoppe has 1,946 part-time openings. This New Jersey-based retailer of nutritional supplements also operates stores in Canada under the name 'VitaPath'. The company provides approximately 8,000 different products through its retail stores and over 20,000 through its online websites. Employees have noted good growth opportunities, progressive policies, and above-average pay.

Chipotle currently offers 1,553 part-time openings. Known for using organic meats throughout its more than 1,500 restaurants, the chain has grown to 37,000 employees since its founding in 1993. While the schedule is described as super flexible, employees mention that the work is fast-paced and requires a lot of effort.

Advantage Sales & Marketing has 1,742 part-time openings. They provide outsourced sales and marketing services to major manufacturers like Johnson & Johnson and Unilever. With more than 65 offices in the US and Canada, the company is recognized for its ability to adapt to market changes and offering a well-thought-out, flexible schedule.

Universal Protection Service is one of the largest providers of security services in the U.S., with 1,219 part-time openings. They offer security solutions for a variety of sectors, including airports and healthcare facilities. Many employees highlight the room for advancement and competitive pay compared to other security firms.

PSA Healthcare, also known as Pediatric Services of America, has 1,295 part-time openings. Headquartered in Atlanta, they provide comprehensive home health services. Employees appreciate the ability to provide compassionate one-on-one care and the flexibility offered by supervisors regarding time-off requests.

sections.title: Reading Passage 3: Fire Safety
sections.description: Questions 29-40 - Types: Flow-chart, Sentence Matching, Diagram Label, Short Answer

passages.title: Fire Safety
content: Fire drills are a big part of being safe in school: They prepare you for what you need to do in case of a fire. But what if there was a fire where you live? Would you know what to do? Talking about fires can be scary because no one likes to think about people getting hurt or their things getting burned. But you can feel less worried if you are prepared.

It's a good idea for families to talk about what they would do to escape a fire. Different families will have different strategies. Some kids live in one-story houses and other kids live in tall buildings. You'll want to talk about escape plans and escape routes, so let's start there.

Know Your Way Out
An escape plan can help every member of a family get out of a burning house. The idea is to get outside quickly and safely. Smoke from a fire can make it hard to see where things are, so it's important to learn and remember the different ways out of your home. How many exits are there? How do you get to them from your room? It's a good idea to have your family draw a map of the escape plan. It's possible one way out could be blocked by fire or smoke, so you'll want to know where other ones are. And if you live in an apartment building, you'll want to know the best way to the stairwell or other emergency exits.

Safety Steps
If you're in a room with the door closed when the fire breaks out, you need to take a few extra steps:

• Check to see if there's heat or smoke coming in the cracks around the door. (You're checking to see if there's fire on the other side.)
  • If you see smoke coming under the door — don't open the door!
  • If you don't see smoke — touch the door. If the door is hot or very warm — don't open the door!
  • If you don't see smoke — and the door is not hot — then use your fingers to lightly touch the doorknob. If the doorknob is hot or very warm — don't open the door!
  • If the doorknob feels cool, and you can't see any smoke around the door, you can open the door very carefully and slowly. When you open the door, if you feel a burst of heat or smoke pours into the room, quickly shut the door and make sure it is really closed. If there's no smoke or heat when you open the door, go toward your escape route exit.

subTasks.instruction: Type 9 - Flow-chart Completion: Complete the PASS method. Write ONE WORD ONLY.

flowchart.text: The PASS method is a simple four-step technique for using a fire extinguisher. It stands for Pull, Aim, Squeeze, and Sweep. This method helps people remember the correct sequence of actions when using a fire extinguisher to put out a small fire.
flowchart.caption: PASS Fire Extinguisher Method

questions.text: Step 1: ______ the pin.
Step 2: ______ at the base.
Step 3: ______ the handle.
Step 4: ______ from side to side.

subTasks.instruction: Type 10 - Diagram Label Completion: Label the fire escape plan. Write the correct label for each letter.

diagram.text: The building has a main entrance on the ground floor leading to a reception area. The stairwell is located in the center of the building, providing access to all floors. On the second floor, there are two bedrooms: one on the east side and one on the west side. Each bedroom has a door opening to a shared hallway. The hallway connects to the stairwell and also leads to a fire exit at the rear of the building.
diagram.alt: Fire escape plan diagram showing building layout with labeled areas
diagram.caption: Fire Escape Plan - Write the correct label for each letter

sections.title: Task 1: Letter
sections.subtitle: Part 1: Writing
sections.instruction: You should spend about 20 minutes on this task. Write at least 150 words.
sections.prompt: You recently moved to a new city. Write a letter to a friend telling them about your new home and inviting them to visit.
bullets: Explain what you like about the area

sections.title: Task 2: Essay
sections.subtitle: Part 2: Writing
sections.instruction: You should spend about 40 minutes on this task. Write at least 250 words.
sections.prompt: Some people believe that unpaid community service should be a compulsory part of high school programs. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
bullets: Include relevant examples from your knowledge or experience

listening.title: Listening
listening.instructions: You will hear a number of different recordings and you will have to answer questions on what you hear. There will be time for you to read the instructions and questions, and you will have a chance to check your work. All the recordings will be played once only. The test is in 4 parts. At the end of the test, you will be given 10 minutes to transfer your answers to the answer sheet.

sections.subtitle: Part 1: Listening
